# Coverage Review — 2026-07-22 (prerelease evidence)

## Summary

- **Tooling:** `swift test --enable-code-coverage` (swift-tools 6.1, macOS 15, Xcode-beta) + `xcrun llvm-cov export -format=lcov` per test binary + the `symmeet` executable; per-line data merged by `max(hit)` across all binaries.
- **Command:** `swift test --enable-code-coverage` then `xcrun llvm-cov export -format=lcov -instr-profile .build/out/Products/Debug/codecov/default.profdata <binary>` for each of the six `.xctest` bundles and `symmeet`, concatenated.
- **Result:** pass (all 40+ tests in 6 suites pass; no coverage gate configured in CI).
- **Overall:** lines **61.9 %** (4 117 / 6 648 instrumentable production lines). Functions 92.7 %, regions 82.6 % (llvm-cov report on the combined set). Branches are not emitted for Swift — reported as n/a.
- **Patch coverage:** n/a — HEAD `63c3716` equals `origin/main`; no code diff vs the default branch.
- **Baseline:** n/a — no prior release tag exists; there is nothing to delta against.
- **Mutation / assert-density / perf:** n/a — this is a prerelease evidence run; those signals were not requested.
- **Gate:** no repo-configured threshold found in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` or `Makefile`; CI runs `make coverage` and uploads `coverage.lcov` but does not enforce a minimum. Default 80 % bar (skill rule 17) applies → **Coverage/WARN** for the overall gap.

## Metrics

| Area | Instrumentable lines | Covered | Line % | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| `SymMeetCore` | 3 474 | 2 865 | **82.5 %** | 43/48 source files touched |
| `SymMeetCapture` | 637 | 258 | 40.5 % | 8/12 files; `CaptureSession` and audio sources at 0 % |
| `SymMeetMCP` | 615 | 196 | 31.9 % | 4/6 files; `ToolHandlers` at 17.9 % |
| `SymMeetSpeakerKit` | 192 | 111 | 57.8 % | 2/4 files; `SpeakerKitEngine` at 0 % |
| `SymMeetWhisperKit` | 181 | 0 | **0.0 %** | 0/3 files — no tests exercise this target |
| `symmeet` (CLI) | 1 549 | 687 | 44.4 % | 21/22 files; exercised via CLI subprocess tests |
| `SymMeetAgent` | — | — | — | Not a SwiftPM target; not instrumented by `swift test` |
| **Production total** | **6 648** | **4 117** | **61.9 %** | Below the 80 % default bar |
| Test code | 4 920 | 4 857 | 98.7 % | Sanity: tests themselves run |

## Test quality

- **Mutation:** n/a — not run.
- **Assert density:** n/a — not run.
- **AAA:** n/a — not run.
- **Performance:** n/a — not run.

## Findings

- [ ] **[Coverage/WARN] Overall production line coverage is 61.9 %, below the 80 % default bar**
  - **Status quo:** 4 117 of 6 648 instrumentable production lines are covered across all runs. No repo-configured threshold exists; the skill default (80 %) is the applicable gate.
  - **Likely cause:** `SymMeetWhisperKit` (0 %), `SymMeetCapture` (40.5 %), `SymMeetMCP` (31.9 %) and the `symmeet` CLI (44.4 %) pull the average down; `SymMeetCore` at 82.5 % already clears the bar on its own.
  - **Proposed solution:** Prioritise branch/error paths in `SymMeetCapture`, `SymMeetMCP` and the CLI commands (Record/Transcribe/Diarize/Job), and add a basic wrapper-level test for `SymMeetWhisperKit`. Do not lower the bar — grow the suite.
  - **Uncovered:** n/a (cross-cutting)
  - **Effort/Impact:** Medium effort / high impact

- [ ] **[Coverage/WARN] SymMeetWhisperKit has 0 % coverage**
  - **Status quo:** `Sources/SymMeetWhisperKit/WhisperKitEngine.swift` (130 lines), `WhisperKitError.swift` (12 lines), `WhisperKitModelInstaller.swift` (39 lines) have no coverage data from any test binary.
  - **Likely cause:** `SymMeetWhisperKitTests` exists but its tests do not exercise the engine/installer/error paths that are linked into the binary.
  - **Proposed solution:** Add unit tests for `WhisperKitEngine` transcription lifecycle, `WhisperKitModelInstaller` install/list paths and `WhisperKitError` projection, mirroring the `SymMeetSpeakerKitTests` pattern.
  - **Uncovered:** `Sources/SymMeetWhisperKit/WhisperKitEngine.swift` (whole file, anchor: `WhisperKitEngine`), `Sources/SymMeetWhisperKit/WhisperKitModelInstaller.swift` (whole file, anchor: `WhisperKitModelInstaller`), `Sources/SymMeetWhisperKit/WhisperKitError.swift` (whole file, anchor: `WhisperKitError`)
  - **Effort/Impact:** Medium effort / high impact

- [ ] **[Coverage/WARN] MCP ToolHandlers at 17.9 % — the handler dispatch surface is largely untested**
  - **Status quo:** `Sources/SymMeetMCP/ToolHandlers.swift` has 391 instrumentable lines, only 62 covered (17.9 %). `JSONRPC.swift` is at 53.5 %, `MCPServer.swift` at 53.8 %.
  - **Likely cause:** Existing MCP tests cover transport/schema/safety but not the per-tool handler bodies.
  - **Proposed solution:** Add handler-level unit tests for each registered tool, especially the privacy-sensitive ones (recording, transcript access), exercising success and error paths.
  - **Uncovered:** `Sources/SymMeetMCP/ToolHandlers.swift` (anchor: tool-handler functions), `Sources/SymMeetMCP/JSONRPC.swift` (anchor: `JSONRPC`), `Sources/SymMeetMCP/MCPServer.swift` (anchor: `MCPServer`)
  - **Effort/Impact:** Medium effort / high impact (MCP is a user-facing protocol surface)

- [ ] **[Coverage/WARN] CaptureSession and audio sources at 0 %**
  - **Status quo:** `Sources/SymMeetCapture/CaptureSession.swift` (167 lines, 0 %), `ScreenAudioSource.swift` (56 lines, 0 %), `MicrophoneAudioSource.swift` (56 lines, 0 %) — the actual hardware-capture layer has no coverage.
  - **Likely cause:** These types require `AVCaptureSession` / screen-capture runtime and cannot be exercised without hardware or a test seam.
  - **Proposed solution:** Introduce a protocol seam around `AVCaptureSession`/`CMSampleBuffer` acquisition so a fake can drive the state-machine paths, or gate these as known-uncovered hardware paths in a coverage-exclude list.
  - **Uncovered:** `Sources/SymMeetCapture/CaptureSession.swift` (anchor: `CaptureSession`), `Sources/SymMeetCapture/ScreenAudioSource.swift` (anchor: `ScreenAudioSource`), `Sources/SymMeetCapture/MicrophoneAudioSource.swift` (anchor: `MicrophoneAudioSource`)
  - **Effort/Impact:** High effort / medium impact

- [ ] **[Coverage/WARN] Privacy authorization has good coverage but CapturePermissionService is 50 %**
  - **Status quo:** `RecordingAuthorization.swift` is at 95.5 % (uncovered: lines 18, 66, 77 — likely error/edge branches), `ConsentRecord.swift` and `RetentionPolicy.swift` at 100 %. `CapturePermissionService.swift` is at 50.0 % — the system-permission queries and prompt paths are partially untested.
  - **Likely cause:** Permission paths depend on system authorization status; only the fake/in-memory branches are exercised.
  - **Proposed solution:** Cover the remaining `RecordingAuthorization` branches and add permission-state fixture tests for `CapturePermissionService` (denied/restricted/notDetermined/authorized matrix).
  - **Uncovered:** `Sources/SymMeetCore/Privacy/RecordingAuthorization.swift:18,66,77` (anchor: `RecordingAuthorization`), `Sources/SymMeetCapture/CapturePermissionService.swift` (anchor: `CapturePermissionService`, uncovered lines include 19–48 — the system-query and prompt branches)
  - **Effort/Impact:** Low effort / medium impact

- [ ] **[Config] SymMeetAgent (6 files, ~718 LOC) is not a SwiftPM target and has no coverage**
  - **Status quo:** `Sources/SymMeetAgent/` contains `SymMeetAgentApp.swift`, `RecordingMenu.swift`, `AgentIPC.swift`, `AgentModel.swift`, `ConsentSheet.swift`, `PermissionView.swift` — none appear in any coverage report. The directory is not declared as a target in `Package.swift`.
  - **Likely cause:** SymMeetAgent is likely built as a separate app target (Xcode project or another build invocation) and is not part of `swift test`.
  - **Proposed solution:** Either add it as a SwiftPM target with a test bundle, or document it as out-of-scope for `swift test` coverage and exclude it explicitly from coverage reports.
  - **Uncovered:** n/a (target not instrumented)
  - **Effort/Impact:** Medium effort / medium impact

## Non-issues

- `Sources/SymMeetCore/Engines/TranscriptionEngine.swift`, `Sources/SymMeetCore/Export/ExportFormat.swift`, `Sources/SymMeetCore/SymMeetCore.swift`, `Sources/SymMeetMCP/SymMeetMCP.swift`, `Sources/SymMeetMCP/ToolSchemas.swift`, `Sources/symmeet/Output/EmbeddedRelease.swift` — appear absent from coverage data; likely enum/namespace or pure-type files with no executable lines to instrument.
- Vendored dependencies under `.build/checkouts/argmax-oss-swift/` (WhisperKit, SpeakerKit, ArgmaxCore) appear in the symmeet-binary coverage but are intentionally excluded from the production totals — they are third-party code, not ours to test.
- Generated test entry points / resource bundle accessors under `.build/out/Intermediates.noindex/` are framework boilerplate and excluded.

## Next step

Run `/02-gh-plan --from-coverage .github/coverage/2026-07-22T18-59-17Z.md` to create GitHub issues from the findings above.
